(born 8 March 1942, [Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 方兆祥), a native of [Anqing](/wiki/Anqing "Anqing"), [Anhui Province](/wiki/Anhui_Province "Anhui Province"), is a Chinese politician.[[1]](#cite_note-n961-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Chinese politician

**Fang Zhaoxiang** (born 8 March 1942, [Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 方兆祥), a native of [Anqing](/wiki/Anqing "Anqing"), [Anhui Province](/wiki/Anhui_Province "Anhui Province"), is a Chinese politician.[[1]](#cite_note-n961-1)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fang_Zhaoxiang&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Fang Zhaoxiang obtained a degree in plant protection from [Anhui Agricultural College](/w/index.php?title=Anhui_Agricultural_College&action=edit&redlink=1 "Anhui Agricultural College (page does not exist)") [[zh](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%AE%89%E5%BE%BD%E5%86%9C%E5%AD%A6%E9%99%A2 "zh:安徽农学院")], inside the Department of Agronomy. In March 1966, he assumed a teaching position at Anhui Agricultural College. He became a member of the [Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party "Chinese Communist Party") in December 1971.[[1]](#cite_note-n961-1) In September 1983, he assumed the position of deputy secretary of the CCP Anqing Municipal Committee. In January 1988, he assumed the position of secretary of the CCP Anqing Municipal Committee.[[2]](#cite_note-b824-2) In December 1991, he assumed the role of Secretary General of the CCP Anqing Municipal Committee. In January 1992, he was appointed as a member of the Standing Committee of the [CCP Anhui Provincial Committee](/wiki/CCP_Anhui_Provincial_Committee "CCP Anhui Provincial Committee") and served as the secretary-general of the provincial party committee. In September 1993, he assumed the role of deputy secretary of the [CCP Anhui Provincial Committee](/wiki/CCP_Anhui_Provincial_Committee "CCP Anhui Provincial Committee"). In September 1993, he was appointed Deputy Secretary of the [CCP Anhui Provincial Committee](/wiki/CCP_Anhui_Provincial_Committee "CCP Anhui Provincial Committee"); in January 2001, he assumed the role of Chairman of the [Anhui Provincial Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference](/wiki/Anhui_Provincial_Committee_of_the_Chinese_People%27s_Political_Consultative_Conference "Anhui Provincial Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference").[[2]](#cite_note-b824-2)

In October 2006, he was appointed deputy director of the [Committee of Education, Science, Culture, Health and Sports](/wiki/Committee_of_Education,_Science,_Culture,_Health_and_Sports "Committee of Education, Science, Culture, Health and Sports") of the [10th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference](/w/index.php?title=10th_National_Committee_of_the_Chinese_People%27s_Political_Consultative_Conference&action=edit&redlink=1 "10th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (page does not exist)") (CPPCC), and in March 2008, he was re-elected as deputy director of the Committee of Education, Science, Culture, Health and Sports of the 11th National Committee of the CPPCC.[[3]](#cite_note-t301-3)[[4]](#cite_note-n690-4)

He is delegate to the [14th](/wiki/14th_National_Congress_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "14th National Congress of the Chinese Communist Party") and [16th CCP National Congress](/wiki/16th_CCP_National_Congress "16th CCP National Congress"), delegate to the [9th National People's Congress](/wiki/9th_National_People%27s_Congress "9th National People's Congress"), Member of the 9th, 10th, and 11th [National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference](/wiki/Chinese_People%27s_Political_Consultative_Conference "Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference"). [[5]](#cite_note-z269-5)
